callout.text: Read columns to grade a discipline
Heatmap = WHICH. Gantt = WILL we hit the date.

narration.text: Fifty-three projects. Ten disciplines. One screen. This is the Portfolio Heatmap. Every row is a project. Every column is a discipline — Sales, Founders Office, Design, 3D, MEP, QS, IT, Procurement, Inventory, and Project Cost. Before you read it, learn its language. Green is healthy — eighty to a hundred. Amber is at risk. Red is critical. Grey is no data. The dots flag overdue or blocked tasks. Here's a winner. Cascading Tasks Innovations, Sales discipline, score one hundred, two of two tasks done. That single green cell is a team that closed it out. Slide one cell right and the story flips. Red across Founders Office, Design, and 3D — same project, four disciplines blocked. That's where the standup starts. Read a row to grade a project. Read a column to grade a discipline. One Sales team across fifty-three engagements — at a glance. And the Heatmap is just one lens. Five tabs — Heatmap, Gantt, Kanban, Live Flow, and Scope — every one a different cut of the same fifty-three projects. Slice by time — last thirty days, ninety, six months, a year, or any custom window. Same wall, different season. Search a project, filter by status, narrow to a date range. Three controls, fifty-three projects, one focused view. Pro tip. The Heatmap tells you WHICH project needs you. The Gantt tells you WILL you hit the date. Different questions. Different views. Same data. Open the Heatmap every morning. Reds first, ambers second, greens to celebrate. Five minutes — and you know exactly where the day goes. Fifty-three projects. Ten disciplines. Five views. One Pipeline Tracker — your management hub.
